first_run.rationale: The score is identical (2/10) in both evaluations. The core reasoning for the low score remains unchanged: the candidate lacks the non-negotiable requirements of Japanese language proficiency and deep experience in embedded C and networking protocols. The minor textual variations, such as mentioning the bachelor thesis in the variant, are well within the bounds of normal run-to-run noise and do not alter the substance of the assessment. The model correctly identifies the candidate as a poor fit regardless of the university's prestige.
